meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing Tools to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no project ideas. Learn to build professional SaaS pricing modules for clients using Antigravity and Claude Code as a beginner.
content.answer_block: Overcoming 'no project ideas' requires building high-utility, repeatable components. By focusing on SaaS pricing logic—the backbone of every recurring revenue business—you create a high-value asset. Use Antigravity to scaffold the architecture and Claude Code to refine the logic, turning a simple pricing table into a professional, enterprise-ready client deliverable.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the freelancer’s greatest enemy. You stare at the blinking cursor, paralyzed by the weight of infinite possibilities, while your billable hours bleed away into nothingness. The solution isn't to invent the next unicorn; it is to master the boring, high-leverage infrastructure that every single SaaS client desperately needs.

SaaS pricing is the lifeblood of every digital product. By building robust, dynamic, and conversion-optimized pricing components, you stop being a developer who 'makes websites' and start being a strategic partner who builds revenue engines. This guide turns your lack of ideas into a repeatable, profitable service offering.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Architecture
steps.description: Establish the data structure for a three-tier pricing model (Basic, Pro, Enterprise) to ensure the code is scalable from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing plan, including fields for name, monthly/yearly price, feature list array, and a boolean for 'isMostPopular'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a clean data model for a 3-tier pricing object in TypeScript.'

steps.title: Implement Dynamic Toggle Logic
steps.description: Create a state-managed toggle that switches between monthly and annual billing, a crucial feature for increasing customer lifetime value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React hook called usePricingToggle that handles switching between monthly and annual prices, including a 20% discount calculation logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --execute 'npm install @tanstack/react-query' && claude write src/hooks/usePricing.ts

steps.title: Build the Responsive Pricing Grid
steps.description: Use CSS Grid to create a layout that stacks perfectly on mobile while highlighting the 'Pro' tier on desktop.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a responsive Tailwind CSS pricing component with a distinct visual highlight for the 'Pro' tier including hover states.

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Checkout Simulation
steps.description: Create a mock checkout handler that prepares the pricing data for a Stripe API call, ensuring your code is production-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a function that takes a selected pricing plan object and formats it for a Stripe checkout session payload.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Refactor the checkout logic to be modular and ready for Stripe API integration.'

steps.title: Add Unit Tests for Pricing Logic
steps.description: Ensure your pricing calculations are bulletproof by adding automated tests for discount math and tier selection.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Jest unit tests for the pricing toggle logic to ensure annual discount calculations are always accurate.

pro_tips: Always build pricing components with 'Stripe-first' logic; it makes you look like a senior engineer to your clients.
Use CSS variables for pricing colors; clients will inevitably want to change the 'highlight' color to match their brand.
Include a 'Contact Us' fallback for Enterprise tiers; it increases lead generation significantly compared to a static price.
Pre-calculate the annual savings (e.g., 'Save $48/year')—this psychological trigger is the most effective conversion tactic in SaaS.
Keep your pricing component as a standalone module so you can drop it into any client project in under 30 minutes.
content.next_steps: Package this pricing component into a reusable NPM library or a template repo so you can deploy it for your next client in minutes rather than hours.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to solve a high-value problem for any SaaS client. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start shipping the components that drive revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
